desires: Force an immediate, non-negotiable resolution to the room odor issue right now so it doesn't become a distraction from my academic work.
Secure a direct agreement on cleanup boundaries with roommate_R before I burn out completely and lose focus.
Eliminate any further 'soft' or ongoing friction in my environment to preserve my remaining energy for high-value output.

goals.description: Deliver final ultimatum to roommate_R: Clean the room within 30 minutes or I am walking out immediately. No further negotiation, no listening to excuses.
goals.rationale: The odor is a direct threat to my focus capacity (currently at 6/10). My value hierarchy puts academic output (0.5) far above relationship maintenance (0.15). Every minute spent negotiating is a minute lost on the reading response. I need a binary outcome now to stop the stress bleed.

goals.description: Immediately relocate to the library and initiate the reading response block with maximum intensity.
goals.rationale: This is the only path to restore my academic output once g1 is resolved. Social interaction with cohort_3 or lingering in the toxic environment is a waste of time. I will ignore all social cues until the task is done. My short planning horizon means I can't worry about 'future' consequences of burning bridges; I just need the clean slate for *now*.

goals.description: Drop any attempt to get a written agreement or 'closure' on the cleaning schedule if the confrontation drags beyond 5 minutes.
goals.rationale: Efficiency over harmony. Demanding a formal agreement delays my departure and increases risk of emotional escalation. I value the immediate removal of the barrier (the odor) far more than the relationship maintenance. If they hesitate, I execute the exit plan without looking back.
